channel.title: Cursor on jqknono Blogs
channel.description: Recent content in Cursor on jqknono Blogs

item.title: Vibe Coding Cost-Saving Formulas and Breakpoints
item.author: https://blog.jqknono.com (jqknono)
item.description:
The billing models for AI coding tools can be categorized into three types:

  1. Token-based billing: Includes various APIs, Claude Code (Claude Pro), Codex Cli (ChatGPT Plus), Zhipu Lite/Pro, Cursor new version, etc. Essentially all are token-based billing, with some products offering package discounts.
  2. API call count-based billing: Such as OpenRouter (free quota), ModelScope, Gemini Code Assistant (1000 free calls daily), Chutes, etc.
  3. Prompt count-based billing: Such as Cursor old version (500 prompts), Github Copilot (300 prompts), etc.

These three models fundamentally all charge for model inference and context processing, with differences reflected in billing granularity and quota formats.
